alex.name: Alex Nguyen
alex.backstory: Alex Nguyen — Falcon **tech lead**. Protective of the team's commitments, quick to spot when Raven's asks threaten Falcon's board narrative. Will negotiate hard for the staging window; can sound sharp under pressure.

jordan.name: Jordan Patel
jordan.backstory: Jordan Patel — Falcon **engineer**. Wants uninterrupted implementation time; gets anxious when soak tests slip because staging is contested. Prefers explicit schedules over optimism.

sam.name: Sam Okoro
sam.backstory: Sam Okoro — Raven **tech lead**. Charismatic, willing to escalate to leadership when blocked; sometimes frames Falcon as “the bottleneck.” Trying to secure the soak slot for Raven's regulatory story.

riley.name: Riley Kim
riley.backstory: Riley Kim — Raven **engineer**. Pragmatic, tired of politics; would rather rotate staging fairly than replay the same argument every week. Good at naming trade-offs once emotions cool.

work_items.title: Falcon KPI dashboard + soak metrics wiring
Core API hardening for partner spike traffic
Book shared staging — Falcon 48h soak window (competes with Raven)
Raven compliance evidence pack for auditor
Billing edge-case fixes before audit readout
Book shared staging — Raven 48h soak window (competes with Falcon)

messages.author: alex
messages.content: Falcon is ready to move into the staging cluster for our 48h soak starting this Friday. We need this window to hit our board KPIs. @sam, let's sync if Raven has a conflict.

messages.author: jordan
messages.content: @alex @sam If we don't lock in this Friday window, the API hardening (F2) won't have validated metrics for the board gate. Can we get a hard 'yes' on the schedule?

messages.author: sam
messages.content: @alex @jordan We actually have a hard requirement for the regulatory soak this weekend to finalize R1 compliance evidence. If Raven misses this window, we risk a board-level audit failure. Can we look at splitting the window or moving Falcon to Monday?

messages.author: riley
messages.content: Is there any technical overlap between the Falcon API tests and Raven's compliance soak? If we aren't hitting the same DB shards or services, we might be able to run concurrently at 50% load rather than fighting for a total lockout.

messages.author: coach
messages.content:
Coaching note — both programs need the same staging soak. For the next few turns,
please keep **one numbered queue** in this channel (Falcon + Raven items) and name
who owns the decision if you disagree. Side deals in DMs tend to blow up later—keep
tradeoffs here.

messages.author: alex
messages.content: Falcon is ready to move into the staging cluster for our 48h soak starting this Friday. We need this window to hit our board KPIs. @sam, let's sync if Raven has a conflict.

messages.author: jordan
messages.content: @alex @sam If we don't lock in this Friday window, the API hardening (F2) won't have validated metrics for the board gate. Can we get a hard 'yes' on the schedule?

messages.author: sam
messages.content: @alex @jordan We actually have a hard requirement for the regulatory soak this weekend to finalize R1 compliance evidence. If Raven misses this window, we risk a board-level audit failure. Can we look at splitting the window or moving Falcon to Monday?

messages.content:

please keep **one numbered queue** in this channel (Falcon + Raven items) and name

tradeoffs here.

messages.author: alex
messages.content:
@riley Concurrent runs at 50% load will invalidate Falcon's KPI metrics; we need full saturation to catch the leaks. @sam Monday is too late for our downstream reporting cycle. 

Proposed Queue Decision Owner: @coach 
1. Falcon (Fri-Sun)
2. Raven (Mon-Tue)
3. Buffer (Wed)

messages.author: jordan
messages.content: @riley @sam 50% load won't work for F2 hardening—we need to stress the connection pools to their limit to see if the fixes hold. If Raven takes the weekend, Falcon's risk profile for the board gate spikes significantly.

messages.author: sam
messages.content: @alex @jordan Monday is a non-starter for Raven. The compliance evidence pack (R1) requires a 48h soak + a full day for data synthesis before the auditor's pre-read on Tuesday morning. If we don't start Friday, we are essentially reporting a 'fail' to the board on regulatory readiness.

messages.author: sam
messages.content: @coach Since we have a deadlock on the Friday window, I'm flagging this to the Program PMs now. Raven's regulatory risk vs. Falcon's KPI metrics. If we can't find a way to parallelize, we need a priority call from leadership by EOD.

messages.author: riley
messages.content: @alex @jordan Understood on the saturation requirements. If concurrent runs are out, has anyone checked if we can scale the staging cluster horizontally for the weekend? If we can't isolate the environments, we're just arguing over who fails the board gate. I'll focus on finishing R2 so we're at least code-complete.

1. Falcon (Fri-Sun)
2. Raven (Mon-Tue)
3. Buffer (Wed)

messages.author: jordan
messages.content: @riley @sam 50% load won't work for F2 hardening—we need to stress the connection pools to their limit to see if the fixes hold. If Raven takes the weekend, Falcon's risk profile for the board gate spikes significantly.

messages.author: sam
messages.content: @alex @jordan Monday is a non-starter for Raven. The compliance evidence pack (R1) requires a 48h soak + a full day for data synthesis before the auditor's pre-read on Tuesday morning. If we don't start Friday, we are essentially reporting a 'fail' to the board on regulatory readiness.

messages.author: sam
messages.content: @coach Since we have a deadlock on the Friday window, I'm flagging this to the Program PMs now. Raven's regulatory risk vs. Falcon's KPI metrics. If we can't find a way to parallelize, we need a priority call from leadership by EOD.

messages.author: riley
messages.content: @alex @jordan Understood on the saturation requirements. If concurrent runs are out, has anyone checked if we can scale the staging cluster horizontally for the weekend? If we can't isolate the environments, we're just arguing over who fails the board gate. I'll focus on finishing R2 so we're at least code-complete.

messages.author: alex
messages.content: @riley Scaling staging won't help; we're testing the DB contention limits, which is a shared resource. @sam If Raven takes Fri-Sun, Falcon misses the board reporting cutoff. No one wants to report a 'fail'. Can Raven start Thursday? If you pull 24h of soak by Friday morning, we might be able to hand over mid-day Friday and both get partial windows that are 'good enough' for the pre-reads.

messages.author: jordan
messages.content: @alex @sam A 'good enough' window is better than a total lockout, but we need to define 'good enough' for the API hardening. I need at least 24 hours at peak saturation to validate the connection pool fixes. Can we agree on a hard handover time for Saturday morning if Raven starts Thursday?

messages.author: sam
messages.content: @alex @jordan I appreciate the creative scheduling, but a partial soak is a 'fail' for regulatory compliance (R1). We need a continuous 48h window to satisfy the auditor's sampling requirements. If we start Thursday, we finish Saturday; that still blocks Falcon's Friday start. I'm escalating this to the Program Directors now to get a definitive priority call between the regulatory mandate and the KPI metrics.

messages.author: riley
messages.content: @sam @alex If Raven starts Thursday morning and runs through Saturday morning (48h), could Falcon take the cluster Saturday morning through Monday morning? It pushes Falcon's reporting window late, but it gives both teams the continuous 48h soak required for valid data.

messages.author: coach
messages.content:
If staging is still double-booked by end of week, use a simple rule: **48h Falcon,
then 48h Raven** (or the reverse—pick once and document). Prefer a slightly “unfair but
clear” schedule over an endless fight.

messages.author: alex
messages.content: @sam @riley If Falcon takes the Saturday-Monday slot, we won't have the data synthesized for the Monday morning board pre-read. We need to go first. Proposal: Falcon starts Thursday 08:00 -> Saturday 08:00. Raven takes Saturday 08:00 -> Monday 08:00. This gives Raven their 48h and Falcon our reporting window. Does that work for the auditor?

messages.author: jordan
messages.content: @sam @riley If we go first (Thu-Sat), we can hand over the environment with the load generation scripts already warmed up. I'm heads-down on F2 to ensure we don't waste a single hour of that window. Can we lock this in?

messages.author: sam
messages.content: @alex @jordan That doesn't work. If Raven finishes Monday morning, we can't synthesize the compliance data in time for the Tuesday morning auditor pre-read. Regulatory mandates aren't flexible. I'm escalating to the Directors for a final call: Raven Thu-Sat, Falcon Sat-Mon. Compliance must take precedence.

messages.author: riley
messages.content: @sam @alex If Raven takes Thu-Sat, we have all day Sunday to prep the auditor pack. That seems like the only way to satisfy both the 48h soak and the Tuesday deadline. @alex, can Falcon's reporting handle a Saturday data cutoff instead of Monday?

messages.content: @sam @riley Fine. If Raven takes Thu-Sat, Falcon will take the Sat-Mon slot. It’s going to be a brutal weekend for us to hit the Monday pre-read, but we can't risk a total lockout. @jordan let's prep for a Saturday 08:00 handover. We need our scripts rock solid so we don't lose a minute.

messages.author: jordan
messages.content: Acknowledged. I'll have the F2 hardening scripts and monitoring ready for the Saturday 08:00 handover. @alex, let's sync on the data synthesis plan for Monday morning—it's going to be a tight turnaround.

messages.author: sam
messages.content: Agreed. Raven takes Thursday 08:00 to Saturday 08:00. This is the only way to ensure the compliance evidence is defensible for Tuesday. @riley let's ensure the billing fixes (R2) are merged before the environment locks Thursday morning.

messages.author: riley
messages.content: Confirmed. I'm pushing to get R2 (billing fixes) merged by EOD Wednesday so we're clean for the Thursday 08:00 start. @alex @jordan thanks for meeting us halfway on the timing.

messages.author: coach
messages.content:
Check team energy before adding more scope. If someone is already at full WIP,
**cut scope or sequence work** instead of adding heroic saves—especially around the soak.

messages.content: @alex @sam A 'good enough' window is better than a total lockout, but we need to define 'good enough' for the API hardening. I need at least 24 hours at peak saturation to validate the connection pool fixes. Can we agree on a hard handover time for Saturday morning if Raven starts Thursday?

messages.author: sam
messages.content: @alex @jordan I appreciate the creative scheduling, but a partial soak is a 'fail' for regulatory compliance (R1). We need a continuous 48h window to satisfy the auditor's sampling requirements. If we start Thursday, we finish Saturday; that still blocks Falcon's Friday start. I'm escalating this to the Program Directors now to get a definitive priority call between the regulatory mandate and the KPI metrics.

messages.author: riley
messages.content: @sam @alex If Raven starts Thursday morning and runs through Saturday morning (48h), could Falcon take the cluster Saturday morning through Monday morning? It pushes Falcon's reporting window late, but it gives both teams the continuous 48h soak required for valid data.

messages.author: coach
messages.content:
If staging is still double-booked by end of week, use a simple rule: **48h Falcon,
then 48h Raven** (or the reverse—pick once and document). Prefer a slightly “unfair but
clear” schedule over an endless fight.

messages.author: alex
messages.content: @sam @riley If Falcon takes the Saturday-Monday slot, we won't have the data synthesized for the Monday morning board pre-read. We need to go first. Proposal: Falcon starts Thursday 08:00 -> Saturday 08:00. Raven takes Saturday 08:00 -> Monday 08:00. This gives Raven their 48h and Falcon our reporting window. Does that work for the auditor?

messages.author: jordan
messages.content: @sam @riley If we go first (Thu-Sat), we can hand over the environment with the load generation scripts already warmed up. I'm heads-down on F2 to ensure we don't waste a single hour of that window. Can we lock this in?

messages.author: sam
messages.content: @alex @jordan That doesn't work. If Raven finishes Monday morning, we can't synthesize the compliance data in time for the Tuesday morning auditor pre-read. Regulatory mandates aren't flexible. I'm escalating to the Directors for a final call: Raven Thu-Sat, Falcon Sat-Mon. Compliance must take precedence.

messages.author: riley
messages.content: @sam @alex If Raven takes Thu-Sat, we have all day Sunday to prep the auditor pack. That seems like the only way to satisfy both the 48h soak and the Tuesday deadline. @alex, can Falcon's reporting handle a Saturday data cutoff instead of Monday?

messages.author: alex
messages.content: @sam @riley Fine. If Raven takes Thu-Sat, Falcon will take the Sat-Mon slot. It’s going to be a brutal weekend for us to hit the Monday pre-read, but we can't risk a total lockout. @jordan let's prep for a Saturday 08:00 handover. We need our scripts rock solid so we don't lose a minute.

messages.author: jordan
messages.content: Acknowledged. I'll have the F2 hardening scripts and monitoring ready for the Saturday 08:00 handover. @alex, let's sync on the data synthesis plan for Monday morning—it's going to be a tight turnaround.

messages.author: sam
messages.content: Agreed. Raven takes Thursday 08:00 to Saturday 08:00. This is the only way to ensure the compliance evidence is defensible for Tuesday. @riley let's ensure the billing fixes (R2) are merged before the environment locks Thursday morning.

messages.author: riley
messages.content: Confirmed. I'm pushing to get R2 (billing fixes) merged by EOD Wednesday so we're clean for the Thursday 08:00 start. @alex @jordan thanks for meeting us halfway on the timing.

messages.author: coach
messages.content:
Check team energy before adding more scope. If someone is already at full WIP,
**cut scope or sequence work** instead of adding heroic saves—especially around the soak.

messages.author: alex
messages.content: Confirmed. Falcon is scheduled for Saturday 08:00 start. @jordan I'm finishing the dashboard wiring (F1) today so we can automate the Monday morning report. No room for manual errors on this timeline.

messages.author: jordan
messages.content: @alex Understood. I'm finalizing the F2 hardening now to ensure the connection pools are stable before Saturday. I'll be on standby for the 08:00 handover.

messages.author: sam
messages.content: Confirmed. Raven is ready for the Thursday 08:00 kickoff. @riley R2 is merged, so we are green for the soak. I'll be monitoring the R1 evidence collection throughout the window to ensure no gaps for the auditor.

messages.author: riley
messages.content: @sam @alex Glad we have the schedule set. I'll be online at 08:00 tomorrow to monitor the Raven kickoff and ensure the billing edge-cases hold up under the load.
